name: Buttle (Derelict)
description: A diabolical conglomeration, the Buttle maintains the shipping and transport potential of the Shuttle while obtaining the weapons and speed of the Berserker, the two ships it's created from.

name: Cleaver (Derelict)
description: The Cleaver is essentially a more powerful version of the Sunder, made by a small company on Shroud and distributed to a few other select planets. The designers of ALR Manufacturing had great plans to show who the Cleaver belonged to, but the builders ended up deciding to use parts from the original Sunder, and the ship did not look nearly as colorful as the original concept dictated. As the shipyards use imported products such as the Tractor Beam, the Cleaver has a rather high price tag. 	Following the tradition, the Cleaver has a roomy cargo bay; enough to make anyone flying it rich. 	According to some, it also carries special tools that allow it to harvest resources from the surface of mineral-rich planets or moons.

name: Cutlass (Derelict)
description: This ship is a modified version of a captured Navy Gunboat. Although there is little most pirates can do to improve the already advanced shielding, they can still weld on a few extra hull reinforcements and an extra pair of guns. This gives them a tough and reasonably fast gun platform to quickly attack, plunder, and escape.

name: Dragonfly (Derelict)
description: The Dragonfly was a light transport, designed for bringing a small number of very rich passengers - Syndicate CEOs, for example - from one planet to another with speed and style. It makes up for its light armament by being able to outrun nearly every other ship in the sky. 	That is, it was a light transport until a scheming Megaparsec engineer came up with a combination of the Arrow and the Wasp, at which point it became something much deadlier. While still not strong enough for close combat, the Dragonfly can easily defeat much larger enemies by simply staying away and bombarding them with missiles.

name: Recruiter (Derelict)
description: When the Headhunter saw a huge success in sales, the feedback polls that Lionheart sent out only found one problem - the hull was too fragile. Their newer iteration, called the Recruiter, remedies the hull issues, although it had to lose a turret mount to do so, and the shield matrices are much less advanced.

name: Rumskib Marauder Behemoth (Derelict)
description: The Behemoth is a recent ship design from Betelgeuse Shipyards, designed as an alternative to the Syndicate Bulk Freighter. Although its cargo capacity is somewhat lower, it is also more maneuverable, and packs a heavier armament by default. 	This Marauder version has been heavily modified and is slower with a greater hull strength.

name: Tanager (Derelict)
description: The Tanager is a new model of transport ship from Tarazed Shipyards. More nimble compared to models of shuttlecraft from Betelgeuse, it is also supplemented with a greater capacity for defensive armaments; a necessity in the region of space that surrounds Tarazed. 	The Tanager also comes with a decent amount of free space, allowing for a much greater level of customization.

name: Buttle (Northern)
description: A diabolical conglomeration, the Buttle maintains the shipping and transport potential of the Shuttle while obtaining the weapons and speed of the Berserker, the two ships it's created from.

name: Cleaver (Defense)
description: The Cleaver is essentially a more powerful version of the Sunder, made by a small company on Shroud and distributed to a few other select planets. The designers of ALR Manufacturing had great plans to show who the Cleaver belonged to, but the builders ended up deciding to use parts from the original Sunder, and the ship did not look nearly as colorful as the original concept dictated. As the shipyards use imported products such as the Tractor Beam, the Cleaver has a rather high price tag. 	Following the tradition, the Cleaver has a roomy cargo bay; enough to make anyone flying it rich. 	According to some, it also carries special tools that allow it to harvest resources from the surface of mineral-rich planets or moons.

name: Modified Argosy (Gat)
description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

name: Ladybug (New Description)
description: These sensor-laden exploration vessels hail back to a more peaceful era of the Hai. Though they have no true ramscoop, a technology the Hai never developed, their built-in collection gills magnify the natural ramscoop present in all ships. These expensive features are the reason the ship has been replaced with the Lightning Bug. However, this ship has seen combat in recent years against human pirates. With room for plentiful shield regeneration, good maneuverability, and the range to strike deep in pirate territory, their long sleep has ended.

name: Splinter (Miner)
description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by the Megaparsec shipyard. It is designed to be faster and more maneuverable than other ships of a similar size, in order to serve as a heavy escort ship or as part of a planetary defense and patrol fleet. Because of its intended use in merchant convoys, it also has a fair amount of cargo space.

name: Rumskib Destroyer (Kitteh)
description: The Republic Destroyer is the newest Navy design, a smaller version of the Cruiser designed to fight toe-to-toe with even the largest pirates. Although it can reach higher top speeds than any of the other large Navy ships, it takes longer to get there - unless it uses its afterburner.

name: Sea Scorpion (Shredder, Jump)
description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

name: Marauder Leviathan (timeout)
description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service for a long time, and captains have had some very interesting ideas about how to modify them for optimum performance. This one has had the hull surface completely stripped and replaced, with the new layout containing more shield projectors per square meter.

name: Shield Beetle (Azure)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Ibis (Zoura)
description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

name: Heliarch Stalker (Zoura)
description: Boasting incredible durability for a ship this size, the Stalker is a compact fortress that can take serious punishment and recover faster than some warships. Its speed and maneuverability, however, leave much to be desired. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Selii'mar (Zoura)
description: The Selii'mar was a common Builder mining drone, one of the few automated ships they ever produced. The Het stations still produce them, in order to harvest the materials they need to keep working. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

name: Cicada (Sanujab)
description: A design nearly as old as the Aphid, practically no new Cicadas have been built in recent memory; it is so reliable that the second-hand market is saturated with Cicadas that have outlived not just one pilot, but in many cases whole lineages of Hai that have owned one and passed it down through the generations. This feature is likely the main factor which has kept the popularity of the Scarab in check as well as defining the Yeer e Ki shipyard's motto: "Service for Life."

name: Scout (Intola)
description: The Lionheart Scoutship was designed back in the days when the galaxy was more sparsely populated, and ships prospecting for habitable worlds or worlds rich in resources would often travel far away from any habitable ports where they would be able to refuel. Although the galaxy has now been much more thoroughly explored, the Scout remains a popular ship for long-distance travel.

name: Splinter (Verdorve)
description: The Splinter is the largest warship produced by the Megaparsec shipyard. It is designed to be faster and more maneuverable than other ships of a similar size, in order to serve as a heavy escort ship or as part of a planetary defense and patrol fleet. Because of its intended use in merchant convoys, it also has a fair amount of cargo space.

name: Shield Beetle (Sookoot)
description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

name: Clipper (Andy Lockman)
description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

name: Saber (Gatling-Built)
description: During the caravan era, a boom of economic development spread along the paths of more heavily armed and technologically advanced merchant fleets originating from the Deep, which resulted in ships such as the Saber being developed. This medium warship was one of the powerhouses of military might in its time, armed to the teeth with five gun ports in the nose and a dorsal turret mount to fire at anything not in front of it. As pirates began to join together in larger war bands in response to the more heavily defended merchants, the Saber became popular amongst them as well. Unlike other ships such as the Firebird, it slowly faded out as competition rose, but it is nevertheless cherished by those who still own one.

name: Sasumata (Angoma)
description: The Sasumata is the chief scout ship of the Bunrodea. While not designed for combat, this stealth transport vessel is still capable of holding its own against some smaller warships; its four fighter bays turn the Sasumata into a force to be reckoned with when its fighters are deployed. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Sasumata's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

name: Telis'het (Human Tech)
description: When the Builders designed the exoskeleton of the Telis, they came to the conclusion that it would have been impossible to make it resilient enough without compromising its agility and integrity. Instead, they focused heavily on improving their shield technology, giving the Telis many of their regenerators.

name: Modified Tachytia (WV-51)
description: This was once an Avgi sublight starship, one of the last models built before the discovery of the hyperdrive. Most starships around this period used ram-augmented fusion drives to reach cruising speeds of up to 0.25c, but the Tachytia class was designed to reach a velocity of 0.4c with an antimatter fuel source to ignite propellant gathered with its huge ramscoop. 	This particular ship was never launched on its mission, commissioned just before the hyperdrive made it obsolete. Now, it has been extensively modified by removing many of its propellant tanks, replacing them with a hyperdrive, additional equipment spaces, and several docking bays. The cryosleep bay has also been substituted for spacious habitatation cylinders, making this a true city ship.

name: Akoustiki (WV-51)
description: The Akoustiki Excursion Vehicle is a modern take on the utility pod, a small personal craft designed to aid in the maintenance and repair of a station, or a carrier vessel and its escorts. It is just as commonly seen flitting about the orbitals as it is servicing satellites or acting as a personal transport shuttle between stations and low gravity bodies. Recent advances in magnetoplasmadynamic engines have made it a capable mining craft as well, albeit one with little cargo space for ores. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

name: Waverider (WV-51)
description: The Waverider was a ship that was first requested and never used for five years, seven months and twenty-seven days. 	Its disappointment was so immense, it willed itself into being.

name: Vyrmeid (Hyperdrive)
description: These are tiny space creatures that feed on solar energy, storing it into two receptors found at the front of their bodies. They are extremely docile, and are found in large groups, almost never seen alone.
